2605.13595.title: Inducing Artificial Uncertainty in Language Models
2605.13595.paper_summary: In safety-critical applications, language models should be able to characterize their uncertainty with meaningful probabilities. Many uncertainty quantification approaches require supervised data; however, finding suitable unseen challenging data is increasingly difficult for large language models trained on vast amounts of scraped data. If the model is consistently (and correctly) confident in its predictions, the uncertainty quantification method may consistently overestimate confidence on new and unfamiliar data. Finding data which exhibits enough uncertainty to train supervised uncertainty quantification methods for high-performance models may therefore be challenging, and will increase in difficulty as LLMs saturate datasets. To address this issue, we first introduce the problem of inducing artificial uncertainty in language models, then investigate methods of inducing artificial uncertainty on trivially easy data in the absence of challenging data at training time. We use probes trained to recognize artificial uncertainty on the original model, and find that these probes trained on artificial uncertainty outperform probes trained without artificial uncertainty in recognizing real uncertainty, achieving notably higher calibration on hard data with minimal loss of performance on easy data.
2605.13595.paper_summary_zh: 在安全關鍵的應用中，語言模型應能以有意義的概率來表徵其不確定性。許多不確定性量化方法需要監督數據；然而，對於在大量抓取數據上訓練的大型語言模型來說，找到合適的未見挑戰性數據變得越來越困難。如果模型對其預測始終（且正確地）充滿信心，那麼不確定性量化方法可能會對新的和不熟悉的數據持續高估信心。因此，找到足夠顯示不確定性的數據以訓練高性能模型的監督不確定性量化方法可能會具有挑戰性，並且隨著大型語言模型飽和數據集，這一挑戰將會加劇。為了解決這個問題，我們首先引入在語言模型中引入人工不確定性的問題，然後研究在訓練時缺乏挑戰性數據的情況下，在簡單數據上引入人工不確定性的方法。我們使用訓練來識別原始模型上人工不確定性的探針，並發現這些在人工不確定性上訓練的探針在識別真實不確定性方面表現優於未經人工不確定性訓練的探針，在困難數據上達到顯著更高的校準，同時在簡單數據上性能損失最小。
2605.13595.author: Sophia Hager et.al.
2605.13595.authors: Sophia Hager, Simon Zeng, Nicholas Andrews
